summarization.a: The figure displays X-ray diffraction patterns of multiple HfO2 samples prepared at different temperatures, showing that growth of crystalline phase(s) does not occur below 200 °C.

a.question: On what substrate were these films grown?
What are the temperatures at which these four samples were grown?
What phase of HfO2 is primarily observed here?
How does temperature affect the refraction patterns?
a.answer: As temperature increases, so too does the intensity of the refraction peaks. At 150 °C, the temperature is too low for crystallization to occur. This is why no refraction peaks were observed at this temperature. The refraction peaks appear at 200 °C and are strongest at 225 °C.
